title: Recess Conditioning
description: You ran laps during recess 'for conditioning.' Other kids are concerned.

choices.text: Keep running. This is the work.
choices.flavor: The teacher asked if you were being punished. You said no. She did not believe you.

choices.text: Recruit others to run with you
choices.flavor: Three kids join. Two quit after one lap. The third keeps up. Accountability partner found.

title: Self-Directed Practice
description: Nobody told you to practice. You went outside and threw a ball against the wall for an hour.

choices.text: Keep going until it gets dark
choices.flavor: Thud. Thud. Thud. The rhythm is meditative. Your arm is sore. You do not stop.

choices.text: Switch to a different drill after a while
choices.flavor: Throwing, then catching, then sprints. A full workout at age 6. Coaches would be impressed.

title: The Shortcut
description: During a race at field day, you notice you could cut a corner and nobody would see.

choices.text: Run the full course. Win it clean.
choices.flavor: You finish second. But you know the truth. And that matters more than a ribbon.
You won. But something feels off. You cannot explain it yet.

title: First Structured Drills
description: An older kid at the park is showing everyone how to do proper football drills.

choices.flavor: You line up, set your feet, and run the route. The older kid says 'not bad.'

choices.text: Try to show off and go beyond the drill
choices.flavor: You add a spin move nobody asked for. It does not work. But the ambition is noted.
